constraints_1_content: Before modifying any source file, use Read to examine its current content in full — do not make edits based on assumptions about the existing code structure.
constraints_1_rationale: This is specific to the React refactoring context where existing file structure matters; requires ClaudeCode's Read tool.
constraints_2_content: Use Grep to search for all occurrences of `inputCount` across the entire project (not just App.js) before making prop corrections, to ensure no stray usages are missed.
constraints_2_rationale: Specific to the prop-name bug fix in this React task; relies on ClaudeCode's Grep tool.
constraints_3_content: Use Glob to confirm the src/ directory layout and verify all four target files (App.js, CustomInput.js, Verification.js, StateDisplay.js) exist before attempting any edits.
constraints_3_rationale: Task-specific file targets; uses ClaudeCode's Glob tool for pre-edit verification.
constraints_4_content: After running `npm install`, use Bash to confirm the installed version with `npm list react-otp-input` and assert it matches `3.1.1` before proceeding to code edits.
constraints_4_rationale: Version validation is specific to the react-otp-input dependency requirement; uses ClaudeCode's Bash tool.
constraints_5_content: Before starting the dev server, use Bash to check if port 3000 is already occupied (e.g., `lsof -i :3000` or `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`) and kill any conflicting process if found.
constraints_5_rationale: Port conflict prevention is specific to the React dev server startup step; uses ClaudeCode's Bash tool.
constraints_8_content: When refactoring CustomInput.js with React.forwardRef, preserve all existing props forwarding and any inline styles or className attributes already present — do not strip functionality while adding the ref forwarding.
constraints_8_rationale: This is a React-specific implementation concern tied to the CustomInput refactor; does not reference ClaudeCode tools.
